title: The 1989 proposal that led to the World Wide Web
content:
On March 12, 1989, computer programmer Sir Tim Berners-Lee wrote a paper titled “Information Management, a proposal.” Working at CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, he had a problem with information about particle accelerators and experiments being stored on

The post The 1989 proposal that led to the World Wide Web appeared first on The Silicon Underground.
